MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Haley Hansen of CMBG3 LAW LLC (415-957-2325), appearing for defendant and moving party Quinn Company.
Aaron D. Costa of SELMAN BREITMAN LLP (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant and opposing party Caterpillar, Inc.

RULING: DEFENDANT QUINN COMPANY'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT AGAINST CATERPILLAR, INC.
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Quinn Company's Motion for Leave to File Cross-Complaint Against Caterpillar, Inc. is denied. "Except as between plaintiffs and defendants, there is no compulsory cross-complaint in California procedure . . .. Rather a cross-complaint is permissive as between co-parties . . .." (Banerian v. O'Malley (1974) 42 Cal.App.3d 604, 612.) If a proposed cross-complaint is permissive, leave of the court may be granted "in the interest of justice" at any time during the course of the action. (CCP 428.50(c).) The Court finds that the interest of justice does not support leave for Quinn Company to file its cross-complaint. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Jennifer Roux of US Legal Support, CSR #11033, jroux@aikenwelch.com (Tel: 415-362-4346) (503/CML)

Hearing held, argued and the Court rules as follows: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(E). Trial date is December 24, 2018 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is January 3, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 20, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on 60 days notice. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 14, 2018. Expert discovery shall remain open until the swearing in of the jury. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than seven days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
